texto: That's the funny thing about children. When they were around, you wanted peace and quiet. A mere moment to yourself. You felt absolutely desperate to go more than three minutes without hearing the word Moooooooom echoing throughout the house. To go to the bathroom or - if luck was really on your side - to take an uninterrupted shower. Yet, when they were absent, no matter how infrequently that happened, it felt as though someone had amputated your limb and left a stinging open wound in its place. And you craved them like a cold beer on a blistering summer day.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Emily Liebert: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “That's the funny thing about children. When they were around, you wanted peace and quiet. A mere …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que familia, children, motherhood pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de motherhood.
